This diff changes the non-fp8 KV-cache page alignment for DeepseekV4 sparse-MLA layers from `None` (no padding) to `512` bytes in five specs (attention.py DeepseekV4Attention + DeepseekV4IndexerCache, compressor.py CompressorStateCache, sparse_swa.py DeepseekV4SWACache). Via `_apply_alignment_padding` in kv_cache_interface.py, `alignment` rounds `real_page_size_bytes` up to a multiple of 512 and stores it in `page_size_padded`, which `page_size_bytes` returns directly. When the true per-block page size is not already a multiple of 512, this pads every KV-cache page, permanently increasing GPU KV-cache footprint and reducing the number of allocatable blocks (fewer concurrent sequences / shorter max context). This is exactly the buffer-sizing-layout mechanism: the buffer size at allocation grows for layout reasons. However, this is an intentional correctness fix (FlashInfer sparse indexes KV by physical token stride, requiring packed 512B-aligned pages), and the accompanying `_remap_flashinfer_index` / `_packed_block_span` logic exists precisely to consume that packed layout. The memory overhead is a deliberate, bounded trade-off for correctness, not an accidental regression.
findings.suggested_benchmark: Measure num_gpu_blocks / KV-cache page_size_bytes for a DeepseekV4 (V3.2 / non-fp8_ds_mla) config before vs after; verify max concurrent sequences and max_model_len at fixed gpu_memory_utilization are not materially reduced by 512B page padding.

The new `record_tool_parser_invocation` is invoked from `DelegatingParser.extract_tool_calls_streaming` (via `_extract_tool_calls_streaming` -> `parse_delta`), which runs once per streaming delta (per generated token/chunk) during auto tool-call streaming. On this per-delta hot path the function does, on every call: (1) two module-level imports of `ChatCompletionRequest`/`ResponsesRequest` (Python `import` statements execute a sys.modules dict lookup on every invocation rather than being hoisted to module top), (2) a `match request:` structural-pattern isinstance check, and (3) a `Counter.labels(...)` lookup which hashes the 4-tuple of label values and does a dict lookup in the prometheus_client metric, followed by `.inc()`. This is repeated small per-call work with no cacheable one-time form beyond the imports, adding constant overhead to every token during streamed tool-call responses.

In _apply_rotary_pos_emb_thd CASE 2 (traditional/max-seqlen freqs mapping), the diff reverts the previously-optimized single-pass application. The parent built one packed freqs tensor with a single torch.cat and invoked _apply_rotary_pos_emb_bshd exactly ONCE on the whole packed tensor t. The new code loops over sequence_splits and calls _apply_rotary_pos_emb_bshd once PER packed sequence, plus an extra output.narrow(...).copy_() per sequence into a pre-allocated buffer. Since _apply_rotary_pos_emb_bshd internally launches several elementwise kernels (torch.cos, torch.sin, _rotate_half's chunk+cat, mul/add, final torch.cat), this multiplies the number of kernel launches from O(1) to O(b) (b = number of packed sequences per batch) on a hot RoPE path executed every attention layer / forward step. This is exactly the per-sequence list-comprehension pattern that the historical THD RoPE fix eliminated, now reintroduced (for CASE 2 only; CASE 1 still applies once).
findings.suggested_benchmark: Microbenchmark _apply_rotary_pos_emb_thd with max-seqlen freqs (CASE 2), cp_size>1, and a packed batch of many short sequences (e.g. cu_seqlens with 32-128 sequences); compare wall time / kernel-launch count of the parent single-apply implementation vs the new per-sequence loop.

The new PrefillPrefixLMAttention.get_kv_cache_spec tags its FullAttentionSpec with non_causal=True, and the added engine-core logic (vllm/v1/engine/core.py) reads this flag to globally disable prefix caching and chunked prefill for any model containing such a layer. HrmTextForCausalLM registers PrefillPrefixLMAttention for every recurrence step across BOTH H and L stacks, so every HRM-Text deployment will run with prefix caching (APC) turned off. This matches the historical 'prefix-state-caching' pattern where a spec/guard forcibly disables prefix caching, causing loss of shared-prefix KV reuse and throughput regression. Unlike the historical cases this is intentional for correctness of the bidirectional Prefix-LM prefill, but the throughput cost (no APC across requests sharing prefixes, no chunked prefill) is real and worth flagging as a scheduling-feature regression scoped to this model family.
findings.suggested_benchmark: Throughput with repeated shared-prefix prompts on HrmTextForCausalLM vs a comparably-sized causal model, measuring cache-hit rate and tokens/s.

findings.claim: HrmTextAttention creates a distinct PrefillPrefixLMAttention instance (each with its own layer_name and dedicated KV cache slot) for EVERY recurrence step: L stack has H_cycles*L_cycles steps and H stack has H_cycles steps, each times num_layers_per_stack. Because these are recurrent re-uses of the same weights but each gets a unique full-attention KV cache allocation, the KV cache footprint scales with the number of recurrence steps rather than with the (small) number of physical layers. This can drastically reduce the number of concurrent sequences that fit in KV cache and thus max batch size / throughput, relative to what the model's parameter count would suggest.
findings.suggested_benchmark: Measure kv_cache_size_tokens / max_concurrency reported at engine init for HrmTextForCausalLM across varying H_cycles/L_cycles and compare achievable concurrent request count.

findings.claim: The diff adds an `ast.parse(candidate)` plus a full `ast.walk(module)` (scanning every AST node for `ast.Set`) inside the shared `make_valid_python()` in vllm/tool_parsers/utils.py. `make_valid_python` is called once per streaming delta by all pythonic-style parsers (lfm2, pythonic, llama4_pythonic, olmo3). Every one of those callers then IMMEDIATELY re-parses the same string: `module = ast.parse(valid_text)` where `valid_text == candidate`. So each streaming delta now parses the entire accumulated tool-call text TWICE and additionally walks the whole tree once. Because streaming re-passes the full current_text on every token delta, this is O(N) per delta over N tokens => O(N^2) parsing work across a tool call, and the walk adds a redundant full-tree traversal on every delta. This is per-iteration repeated work on the token-streaming hot path, and it silently affects the pre-existing pythonic/llama4/olmo3 parsers too, not just LFM2.
findings.suggested_benchmark: Micro-benchmark extract_tool_calls_streaming for pythonic/lfm2 parser feeding a long multi-tool call token-by-token; compare wall time / parse count before vs after this commit; also measure make_valid_python() cost in isolation on a large valid candidate.

The fix maps sampling_params.logprobs == -1 to self.vocab_size in SamplingStates.add_request. Downstream, max_num_logprobs() reduces via np.max over the whole batch (sampler.py:88), so a single request asking for logprobs=-1 sets num_logprobs = vocab_size for the ENTIRE batch's logprob computation. In compute_topk_logprobs (logprob.py:117 fast path / :129 slow path) this triggers torch.topk(logits, vocab_size, dim=-1) over the full [batch_size, vocab_size] logits — effectively a full-vocab sort — plus torch.cat and compute_token_logprobs allocating a [batch_size, 1+vocab_size] FP32 tensor with a triton kernel using PADDED_TOPK=next_power_of_2(vocab_size). This materializes the full [batch_size, vocab_size] logprobs tensor that the kernel comment (logprob.py:78-81) explicitly says it avoids to save GPU memory. For big vocabularies (128k+) and multi-request batches this is a large, batch-amplified compute + memory cost on the hot sampling path. While functionally correct (fixes the crash), the -1 => vocab_size expansion silently makes every co-batched request pay full-vocab topk cost.
findings.suggested_benchmark: Serve with one request setting logprobs=-1 mixed with other concurrent decode requests (large-vocab model, e.g. 128k); measure per-step sampler latency and peak GPU memory vs a baseline batch with small logprobs; verify the full-vocab torch.topk / [batch, 1+vocab_size] allocation cost and its batch-wide amplification.

findings.claim: In `run_output_dispatcher_loop`, every RequestBatch that carries `scheduler_stats` (typically one per engine scheduler step) now unconditionally calls `inner.lora_adapter_states()`, which acquires the request-registry mutex and iterates over ALL currently tracked requests via `self.requests.values().filter_map(|tracked| tracked.lora.as_ref())`, then clones matching adapter-name strings into two fresh `BTreeSet<String>` allocations. For non-LoRA deployments (the common case) `tracked.lora` is always `None`, so the entire per-step iteration + two BTreeSet allocations + the `LoraInfoExporter::update` call produce no output at all — pure wasted work proportional to the in-flight request count, executed every scheduler step. This is a new per-step redundant cost that did not exist at parent (the LoRA info was previously carried by scheduler stats, not derived here). Consider skipping the snapshot entirely when no LoRA requests are tracked (e.g. a cheap `has_lora` counter on the registry), and avoid re-cloning adapter names into fresh BTreeSets every step when the set has not changed.
findings.suggested_benchmark: Engine-core-client output-dispatcher throughput with many in-flight non-LoRA requests: measure per-step cost / lock-hold time of run_output_dispatcher_loop with 0 LoRA adapters and high concurrency (e.g. 2k in-flight requests) before/after this change.

The added uncondit

This diff enables `arbitrary_precision` (and `preserve_order`) on the workspace-wide `serde_json` dependency (rust/Cargo.toml: `serde_json = { version = "1.0.145", features = ["arbitrary_precision", "preserve_order"] }`). This is a *global cargo feature that unifies across the entire workspace*, so every crate that (de)serializes JSON — including hot request/response paths in vllm-text and vllm-chat — is affected, not just the tool-parser roundtrip tests that motivated the change. `arbitrary_precision` makes serde_json represent every JSON number as a boxed string internally: it disables the fast numeric parse/serialize path, forces number tokens to be materialized as `String` (extra allocation per number), and notably changes `Number::as_i64/as_f64/is_i64` semantics (they now re-parse the stored string). For payloads with many numeric fields (e.g. sampling params, logits/token-id arrays serialized as JSON, tool-call arguments), this adds per-number allocation + reparse overhead on hot serialization paths. `preserve_order` additionally switches serde_json::Map from BTreeMap to IndexMap (pulls in indexmap, see Cargo.lock), changing map insertion/lookup characteristics workspace-wide. The feature change is justified for number-spelling fidelity (see tojson_preserves_arbitrary_precision_number_spelling and convert_number rewrite), but the *global* blast radius is a latent perf regression risk on JSON-heavy hot paths.
findings.suggested_benchmark: Microbenchmark serde_json round-trip (parse+serialize) of a representative chat response / tool-call arguments payload with many numeric fields, comparing feature-flags on vs off; also bench SamplingParams serialization.

findings.claim: In `_parse_image_data`, the diff adds a per-item list comprehension that calls `convert_image_mode(normalize_image(item), "RGB")` for every PIL image on the multimodal parse path (runs once per request for every image item). `normalize_image` invokes `ImageOps.exif_transpose` and `convert_image_mode` may allocate a new PIL image + do a full color-mode conversion (convert / rgba_to_rgb). This is guarded (only for `isinstance(item, PILImage.Image)`), and `convert_image_mode` short-circuits when `image.mode == to_mode`, so the cost for already-RGB images is bounded to the isinstance check + an exif_transpose scan. Images loaded via ImageMediaIO.load_bytes already went through normalize_image + mode conversion, so for those inputs this repeats exif_transpose (and a redundant no-op convert), matching the historical `redundant-hot-path-work` pattern where convert_image_mode was applied on every pass. Impact is small and this is a correctness fix for direct-PIL inputs (which bypass ImageMediaIO), so severity is low, but it is per-request repeated work worth confirming against the URL/base64 loading path to avoid double normalization.
findings.suggested_benchmark: Microbenchmark _parse_image_data with a batch of already-RGB PIL images vs raw/EXIF-tagged images, and end-to-end multimodal request latency comparing images loaded via ImageMediaIO (already normalized) to measure the added exif_transpose/convert cost per item.

findings.claim: 此改动触及 async-output-overlap（inference-serving），该类问题历史上主要在 [async scheduling enabled (use_async_scheduling); single-step overlap, Max-Throughput TP1 conc1024; Min-Latency TP8 conc8 (issue-measured), multiprocess executor (TP/PP with MultiprocExecutor); async scheduling / max_concurrent_batches>1 exercises the non_block Future path] 配置下显现；This adds a streaming SSE path to the Rust frontend's `generate` endpoint, incrementally yielding output chunks as tokens arrive rather than collecting the full

Each `/v1/completions` and `/v1/chat/completions` request now calls `validate_token_id_ranges`, which invokes `state.tokenizer_vocab_size()`. For the HF backend this resolves to `HuggingFaceTokenizer::vocab_size()` -> `HfTokenizer::get_vocab_size(true)` (the `true` = with-added-tokens path), which is NOT a cached scalar: HF `tokenizers::Tokenizer::get_vocab_size(true)` rebuilds/merges the added-vocab into the model vocab to count it. This is the exact shape of the historical `len(tokenizer)` regression, where recomputing tokenizer vocab size on a per-request (hot) path added avoidable work. Recommend caching the tokenizer/model vocab sizes once at backend construction (they are immutable) and having `tokenizer_vocab_size()`/`model_vocab_size()` return the cached value, rather than recomputing per request. Severity is only 'suggestion' because it is per-request (not per-token) so the amortized cost is small relative to generation, but the pattern matches the prior and is trivial to avoid.
findings.suggested_benchmark: Micro-benchmark HuggingFaceTokenizer::vocab_size() (get_vocab_size(true)) in a tight loop for a large added-vocab tokenizer (e.g. Qwen/Llama3) and compare to a cached-scalar read; also measure request throughput of /v1/completions with tiny prompts to isolate per-request fixed cost.

findings.claim: _run_training.sh now unconditionally forces NCCL into the slowest deterministic collective configuration for ALL functional tests: NCCL_PROTO=simple (disables LL/LL128 low-latency protocols), NCCL_ALGO=Ring (disables Tree/CollNet/NVLS), NCCL_NVLS_ENABLE=0 (disables NVLink SHARP, which materially accelerates AllReduce on H100/dgx_h100 targets), NCCL_COLLNET_ENABLE=0, and NCCL_TUNER_PLUGIN='' (prevents the EFA tuner from auto-selecting faster algos). Because these env vars did NOT exist in the parent's shell path (only in launch_nemo_run_workload.py), this is a new addition to the torchrun/ft_launcher training path, not merely a move. The regression is directly visible in the updated golden values: gpt_static_inference_cuda_graphs latency 1.4340 -> 1.4873 (~+3.7%), per-step tpot 0.0169 -> 0.0185 (~+9%); gpt_static_inference_tp4_ep4 latency 17.085 -> 17.223 and per-step tpot ~0.0876 -> ~0.0906. Forcing Ring+Simple and disabling NVLS on H100 is the mechanistic cause of these throughput/latency increases.
findings.suggested_benchmark: Compare gpt_static_inference_* tpot/latency with vs without the forced NCCL_ALGO=Ring / NCCL_NVLS_ENABLE=0 on a dgx_h100 node to quantify the AllReduce/AllGather slowdown.
